intro: Account currency is a control point—not an ISO decoration beside a GL line. At State 04 it decides how balances live inside charts: reconciliation scopes, open-item treatments, foreign-monetary populations feeding revaluation, and the forensic stories auditors replay when FX variance escalates. Treating account currency as stylistic guarantees downstream distortion invisible until close or audit depth.
doctrine_statement:
Custody enters accountable ledger identity here.
Weak State 04 governance poisons every downstream measurement narrative.
primary_section_body: ERP postings combine document currencies with account assignments—finance must govern both conversations separately yet jointly.
Intercompany and shared-service models amplify drift when custody matrices lag organizational reality.
Comparison pages isolate adjacent identities so forums argue about objects—not slogans.

pillar_sections.title: What the control point decides
paragraphs: Authorization workflows for assignment changes, migration replay evidence, reconciliation scope definitions, and linkage between commercial obligations and ledger retention currencies.
Account currency control glossary discipline names governance expectations explicitly—not heroic month-end heroics.

steps.title: Primary definition entry
steps.body: Accessible anchor into account currency custody vocabulary.

steps.title: State 04 canonical reference
steps.body: Enterprise posture for account monetary state inside the chain.

steps.title: Defined governance term
steps.body: Lexical specification for account currency control discipline.

steps.title: Versus transaction currency
steps.body: Obligation denomination contrasted with ERP custody.

steps.title: Versus settlement currency
steps.body: Liquidity rails contrasted with ledger retention custody.

steps.title: Versus ledger currency
steps.body: Chart custody contrasted with measurement shell.

pillar_sections.title: Symptoms of weak account currency control
paragraphs: Receivables balances reconcile locally yet fail group scrutiny when custody scopes disagree with contractual denomination.
Revaluation batches ingest wrong populations because foreign versus functional assignments blurred.
Migration retrospectives lack approvals tying old assignments to restated balances—audit evidence thins exactly when regulators demand replay.

pillar_sections.title: Questions proving State 04 governance
questions: Which matrix pairs entity, account range, and custody assignment—and who certifies exceptions?
How does finance prove postings still reconcile after assignment changes retroactively?
Which reconciliation dashboard scopes strictly honor account currency—not blended FX rhetoric?
Where do commercial confirmations tie into custody assignments before auditors ask?

pillar_sections.title: Bridge into configuration, ledger, reconciliation, FX, and risk synthesis

graph_intro: Strengthen custody vocabulary before debating FX outputs that inherited silent fractures.

cards.label: Custody charter
cards.title: Assignments belong to policy—not informal spreadsheets.
cards.body: Exceptions demand signatures and replay scripts.

cards.label: ERP coupling
cards.title: Engines expose custody fields deliberately.
cards.body: Bypassing them trades short-term speed for long-term assurance debt.

control_title: Written custody matrices plus migration replay packs
control_body: Publish assignment authority matrices; require documented replay after migrations; pair reconciliations to explicit custody scopes embedded in close checklist.
risk_label: Risk if account currency stays ‘just configuration’
risk_title: Structural distortion masked by localized balancing
risk_body: Teams celebrate subsystem zeros while group controllers discover unreconciled monetary identities—remediation costs spike nonlinearly.
